weather_report:
Near midnight in Subiaco, the sky is scrubbed clean and quietly luminous, the kind of clear that sharpens the edges of rooftops and sets the constellations like pins on velvet. The air reads cool against the skin, still and uncomplicated, and the UV, at this hour, is nothing at all. It’s a night to fetch a cardigan or light jacket if you step out, to breathe and listen to the small sounds a city makes when it thinks no one is watching.

Through the small hours and toward dawn the forecast keeps it cloudless, so morning is likely to open bright and transparent, the sun gentle but unwavering; UV will start low and rise into the late morning, winter-moderate yet worth a dab of sunscreen if you linger outdoors. Expect a pleasant, crisp feel: layers will serve you well—long sleeves at first, perhaps a scarf before breakfast, easing to something lighter once the day stretches on. Good hours for an unhurried walk to the corner café, a lap around the park before the traffic wakes, or setting out tomorrow’s to-do list at the kitchen table, letting the clear light make easy work feel easier.
